title: That Scene as it relates to OMF
display_title: That Scene as it relates to OMF
wikitext:
[]: "That Scene" as it relates to "OMF"             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Jun 11, 2012 10:17 am Hi guys and gals. I'm a new poster on the forum, but I've been following this site for half a year now (VERY impressed, I might add) and finally have something (that I hope is) relevant to add. Anyway, I was thinking about "That Scene" from the opening of EoE after I read on here last week in one of the forums that someone said they don't understand it's inclusion. It was around that time that I also was reading people's analysis of OMF (with apologies to Reichu; I recall reading that she hates when it's called that) and the general consensus seemed to be that it's far too ambiguous to understand. Unlike a recent poster on here *cough cough* g.christ *cough cough* I make no claims to have found the truth here. However, I'm curious what you guys think about this: In "That Scene," Shinji masturbates to an unknowing Asuka, the object of his desires throughout the series. In OMF, after Asuka seemingly "chooses to return to existence," Shinji strangles her. The two scenes seem unrelated, however... I found this quote (on this site's wiki, actually) and it created an association, at least in my mind. ...but Anno was dissatisfied with Miyamura's renditions of this line. Eventually, Anno asked her a question which described what he was going for with this scene: "Concerning the final line we adopted, I'm not sure whether I should say about it in fact. At last Anno asked me 'Miyamura, just imagine you are sleeping in your bed and a stranger sneaks into your room. He can rape you anytime as you are asleep but he doesn't. Instead, he masturbates looking at you, when you wake up and know what he did to you. What do you think you would say?' I had been thinking he was a strange man, but at that moment I felt disgusting. So I told him that I thought 'Disgusting.' And then he sighed and said, 'I thought as much."

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jun 11, 2012 10:26 am There's a bunch of threads that talk about (specifically or in passing) about the strangling at the end and the "kimochi warui" interview. If you do a search for "strangling Shinji miyamura" or "masturbate miyamura kimochi warui" or some combination of those words, you'll find a ton of posts.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Jun 11, 2012 10:33 am Alright. Thanks for the heads up. Like I said, I've spent some considerable time on here, but I never came across something that married the two scenes. I'll make sure to give them a look.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 11, 2012 10:33 am Thematically I think you're absolutely right, but I'm not sure how that informs what actually happens at the end of EoE. During P3II Asuka references the opening scene and seems to be okay with it (for sufficiently limited values of "okay", at least), and at the end she seems to be specifically referencing Shinji's behavior at the time (i.e. choking her/breaking down/etc) and/or her current state and/or the state of the world. Nothing in the scene suggests a connection to the opening scene, so Miyamura's conversation with Anno just seems really peculiar. (to clarify, its not clear why that opening scene would be on Asuka's mind at the end, since stuff has happened between then and now and Shinji's doing stuff right now that's perfectly disgusting. With all the stuff that's going on in her head why focus on that scene? Why not the kitchen scene, or even scenes unrelated to Shinji?)

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Jun 11, 2012 10:39 am That was a folly on my part. I should have clarified that I was looking at it from a thematic standpoint. I guess I was just trying to make sense of the "wrong" why (that is, the story-telling 'why,' and not the action itself). I will say, however, that during P3II, I'm still not convinced that that's really Asuka whom Shinji is interacting with, or if it's just a one of his skewed perceptions of whom he views her to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 11, 2012 11:26 am Merkaba wrote: I will say, however, that during P3II, I'm still not convinced that that's really Asuka whom Shinji is interacting with, or if it's just a one of his skewed perceptions of whom he views her to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Jun 11, 2012 11:32 am I'll be sure to check out those discussions in particular. I would like to say, though, that the lines about breaking up and things like "leave me alone" and such that we hear from a chorus of Misato, Rei and Asuka in EoE seem, to me at least, to just be Shinji's interpretations of how they made him feel, and not actual lines from those actual people.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 11, 2012 11:40 am Merkaba wrote: I'll be sure to check out those discussions in particular. I would like to say, though, that the lines about breaking up and things like "leave me alone" and such that we hear from a chorus of Misato, Rei and Asuka in EoE seem, to me at least, to just be Shinji's interpretations of how they made him feel, and not actual lines from those actual people.

[]: Re: "That Scene" as it relates to "OMF"             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 11, 2012 11:46 am Merkaba wrote: OMF (with apologies to Reichu; I recall reading that she hates when it's called that)

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Jun 11, 2012 11:55 am That chorus was everyone in the world, as I recall.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jun 11, 2012 12:32 pm Merkaba wrote: If it was the whole world talking, though, why was it only in the voices of Misato, Rei and Asuka?

[]: Re: "That Scene" as it relates to "OMF"             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 11, 2012 12:46 pm Merkaba wrote: In "That Scene," Shinji masturbates to an unknowing Asuka, the object of his desires throughout the series. In OMF, after Asuka seemingly "chooses to return to existence," Shinji strangles her.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 11, 2012 1:14 pm Merkaba: You don't have to use a misleading acronym OR a hyperbolic description. You can call it the "final scene" or suchlike and let the context say the rest. The script doesn't attribute the wall of rejection dialogue to any characters. It's just "Dialogue (OFF)".

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Tue Jun 12, 2012 8:07 am Do we know that it was? I don't know either way since I haven't seen the original script.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 12, 2012 10:56 am Merkaba wrote: I guess that was assumption on my part, but to me it seemed (in the dubbed version] that the only three voices we heard were the three main female characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 13, 2012 4:19 pm I, for one, had no idea until just now that the title "OMF" applies not just to the final scene but to the whole entire 26`. That being the case I would support the notion that the final scene be called something else. EoEoE works fine; that's what I called it on my own long before I heard anyone else call it OMF. "The final scene" is nice too. I think it's important that it be known that the name OMF:INY does not apply only to that scene, too. It can influence interpretations.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 13, 2012 7:25 pm Monk Ed wrote: I, for one, had no idea until just now that the title "OMF" applies not just to the final scene but to the whole entire 26`.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Jun 14, 2012 7:34 pm I always interpreted the English as chapter titles, where the story has moved to a different space a little bit. In episode 12 the title, "She Said, 'Don't Make Others Suffer for Your Personal Hatred'" seems to be a direct reference to the scene preceding. "Rei III" makes more sense halfway through "Tears" after Rei II has exploded.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Jun 14, 2012 11:14 pm I wonder if they meant to title it "One More Final e ". Probably.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jun 15, 2012 12:40 am Warren Peace wrote: I wonder if they meant to title it "One More Final e ". Probably.

Eventually, Anno asked her a question which described what he was going for with this scene: "Concerning the final line we adopted, I'm not sure whether I should say about it in fact. At last Anno asked me 'Miyamura, just imagine you are sleeping in your bed and a stranger sneaks into your room. He can rape you anytime as you are asleep but he doesn't.
